text: When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs. Identifying bugs is the first step; look for unexpected behavior or error messages. A systematic approach helps you fix them efficiently: read your code carefully, check for typos, and test one section at a time. Use debugging tools like breakpoints in your code editor to pause execution and inspect variable values. Logging is another powerful strategy—inserting print statements into your code lets you track its flow and spot where values go wrong. By combining these methods, you can solve problems step by step without getting frustrated.
